learning_outcomes.description: Think and write well about Australian art and its attendant issues on the basis of a familiarity with works of art seen both in class and on class visits to art galleries and institutions.
Exhibit a thorough knowledge of the historical development of art in Australia, and respond to and write about it critically.

assessments.weighting: <dd>30% There are 8 opportunities across the semester to complete your worksheet in class, each worth 6%. Only your best 5 results count, together forming 30% of your final grade.  </dd>

Alternative assessment and accommodations

If you have a Disability Action Plan relevant to this task, please contact the course coordinator as early as possible. Reasonable accommodations can be made for matters documented in a Disability Action Plan (for example, if you are unable to handwrite under timed conditions).

The 3-session buffer described above is intended to cover ordinary absences. A separate make-up session is available only to students who, for a genuine and documented reason, miss more than 3 of the exercises — for example, a sudden or serious illness, or another documented cause requiring a lengthy absence. Missing 3 or fewer sessions is already covered by the best-5-of-8 structure and does not qualify for a make-up.

To be considered for a make-up session or any other alternative arrangement, you must contact the course coordinator with your documentation as soon as possible after the circumstances arise. One make-up session will be offered in the Week 13 lecture slot; if you are eligible, please ensure you are available.
assessments.task_description:
8 worksheets with short questions on aspects of the lecture and readings will be distributed in tutorials from Week 4 to Week 11 across the semester. The worksheets will be completed in person on paper in the last 15 minutes of the tutorial and handed in directly to the tutor at the end of class. Each of the 8 opportunities across the semester is worth 6%. Only your best 5 results count, together forming 30% of your final grade.

Worksheets are graded on quality of engagement with course readings and lecture (4 points) and quality of communication (2 points).

Because only 5 of the 8 writing exercises count towards your final grade, the assessment already builds in the flexibility to miss up to 3 sessions — for illness or any other reason — with no penalty and no documentation required.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit directly to the tutor at the end of your tutorial. As this is an in-person course, you must make yourself available to complete each exercise at the scheduled date, time and venue. Please see Exam Procedures for full information. These are handwritten, closed-book tasks: no written material, electronic material or electronic devices may be used. All such items must remain in your bag or under your desk, and all electronic devices must be switched off or in airplane mode. If a device emits any noise, you may be subject to an allegation of misconduct and the item may be confiscated for the duration of the exercise. You may bring suitable writing implements (in a clear container), water in a clear bottle with the label removed, and disposable earplugs.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description: Students will choose one work of Australian art currently on display at the Queensland Art Gallery and write an 800 word visual analysis of the work that addresses (i) style and formal characteristics (ii) content and subject matter and (iii) the work's historical significance and relevance. Some background research on the artist and associated art movements will be required to complete this exercise. An image of the work and a bibliography of all reference sources must be attached. Footnotes to reference material should be formatted using Chicago Manual of Style. Footnotes and bibliography are not included in the word count.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit via TurnItIn through Blackboard as a Word or PDF document only.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: Research and write an essay of 2500 words on topics to be distributed on Blackboard. Word length does not include footnotes and bibliography. This assignment requires independent research. In addition to the consultation of the set readings and the recommended reading lists, students need to independently source texts so as to demonstrate original research. A separate bibliography must be attached, preferably in Chicago style. The bibliography must contain a minimum of 10 reference texts, with at least five of these to be sourced through the UQ Library. Please put an asterisk next to the texts in your bibliography that you located through the library.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit via TurnItIn through Blackboard as a PDF or Word doc only.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
